review_title: Wordpress - The most used software in my daily routine
description: I have been using wordpress for years, starting back in the day of name.wordpress.com to buuld your owne personal page. My websites have ecommerce embedded with payment solutions that I can manage myself. Plus for my personal sites, I can have a lot of fun creating different designs and add-ons.
author: Patrick S.
pros: Ease of use. I can get a simple page up and running in literally minutes, and an ecommecre site in under an hour. Beyond that, the templates and add-ons make it possible to design really great looking, functional websites across multiple industries.
cons: Free plug ins. Most plugins have the danger of being hacked, especially if not updated. People...it's free for a reason. Purchase the upgrade!
author_profession: Director of Finance and Creative Director

review_title: The best website design tool is also incredible value for money.
description: I was initially going to get a hosted website that would be tied to the host. So glad I retained my independence by using WordPress. The value for money is unbeatable! I have never had need to even call customer support.
author: Mike H.
pros: It was a bit of a learning curve to set up for me with no experience, but there are loads of tutorials on YouTube to help you. Can't get over how easy it them was to get a professional website! It was also easy to see mobile responsiveness which is now my major source of traffic.
cons: The steep learning curve trying to set it up on my own before I found out about the YouTube tutorials.

review_title: Cheap, cheerful, just make sure it's managed for security
description: For the price, with a little attention about security, a managed WP instance is brilliant. That way, you know the patches are always applied and you've got a suite of plugins to scale such as Woo Commerce.
author: Jonathan L.
pros: The best thing about WP is the choice of templates – it's massive. Creating new pages, especially for content based websites is simple. It's straightforward to adapt the design to suit your needs. It's excellent at managing posts, although news/blogs seems an old method now.
cons: The constant security worries, especially from plugins. There are so many cases of WP sites being hacked, that you need to take this seriously from the outset.

review_title: Affordable, Flexible, and SEO-Friendly
description: As the owner of a marketing agency, I’ve worked with a variety of website platforms for our clients. We feel very comfortable working with WordPress. Its always being easy to use, easy to control to perform SEO, and we like the variety of customization options. Our experience has been great.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: What I like the most about WordPress and why we recommend it to most of our clients is its affordability compared to other platforms, and also because it is incredibly user-friendly and offers great quality and functionality.
cons: When we are changing the fonts and their sizes, sometimes and for some reason, the text doesn't pick up the font size and it doesn't appear visible, which force us to do it manually and try to guess the same size that was used before. Its something minor, but if this could be fixed that would be great.

review_title: The Classic is still the best
description: Wordpress has allowed me to create and update a wonderful website for my company. The plug-ins alone provide anything you can of to really make your website shine and serve any purpose that you can imagine. When learning how to use Wordpress, just remember to stick to it and lean on the broader community to help support you.
author: Andrew Z.
pros: Wordpress is a well priced and feature rich suite for website design and page editing. It has been a trusted name in web design for many years and is so widely used, that it's almost synonymous with web creation. Wordpress has a bit of a learning curve compared to some of its competitor, but that curve lends itself to being far more powerful. There is a huge community dedicated to web design and template creation to support you when you need help.
cons: Official customer support isn't as dedicated as the forums and industry-led community itself. Wordpress isn't designed for beginners. If you're going to learn this powerful tool, you need to put the time in.

review_title: Flexible, easy and empowers business.
description: Couldnt recommend it more. If you have no web development knowledge, or if you have been doing it for years, you can very easily get to grips with WordPress to create sites.
author: Jordan W.
pros: How far you can push it. Most people turn their noses up at easy web builders like WordPress but the flexibility, ease of use and how quickly you can build fully customisable sites using templates empowers so many business.
cons: The bloat that can happen with WordPress core files, themes and alot of plugins can make the sites slower, but with careful consideration this can be overcome

review_title: Feels a little old school!
description: It's not really changed in years which is probably a good thing for some people but I think there are slicker, cheaper, easier to use options out there now.
author: Kate W.
pros: It's the old school option for website building, trusted and used by many. There are also a lot of skins available for design at cheap prices.
cons: It's just too behind when it comes to the updates in online website building these days. I felt like I was working on my blog back in 2013 rather than building a professional website.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Too complicated for a first time user.
description: Great software, but complicated and overwhelming for someone who is new to website design. It has many great features but I found that there were additional hidden fees for many of them.
author: Stephanie W.
pros: WordPress had exhaustive features for website building and design. The customer support was good and overall good value for money.
cons: I found the software very complex and ultimately it didn't work out for me as it was too overwhelming for my first time building a website.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: WordPress is the Easiest and Most Supported CMS
description: I use wordpress everyday to update and add content to my client's website. We are now ranking organically for many key phrases.
author: Sarah G.
pros: WordPress is the easiest and most customizable open website programming system. It allows me to choose from thousands of plugins to accomplish anything you could ever want a website to do and all for free. If I ever have any issues I can usually find a solution online from someone else who already fixed it or found a work around.
cons: The frequent updated can sometimes cause issues for my website or plugins without my knowledge so I test the sites each morning on web and mobile versions.

review_title: Flexible, Powerful, and Easy to Grow With
description: I have been using WordPress for quite some time and am very satisfied with it. It is one of the most flexible platforms I have worked with and makes it easy to build and manage websites of different sizes. The system is intuitive enough for regular content updates, but also powerful enough to support more advanced needs through themes, plugins, and customization options. What I value most is that WordPress gives me a lot of control while still remaining accessible for everyday use.
author: Sven S.
pros: WordPress offers an excellent balance of ease of use and flexibility. It is simple to publish and update content, and the large selection of themes and plugins makes it possible to adapt a website to almost any requirement. The platform is widely supported, regularly updated, and suitable for blogs, business websites, and more complex projects. Its strong community and ecosystem make it easy to find help, extensions, and solutions when needed.
cons: One downside is that the many available plugins can sometimes lead to extra maintenance, and not every extension works perfectly with every theme or update. For beginners, the amount of choice and customization options can feel a bit overwhelming at first. Performance can also depend heavily on the quality of the hosting environment and the plugins being used. Even so, these are manageable trade-offs for the level of freedom WordPress provides.

review_title: Wordpress is great, but...
description: Wordpress was fine. I cannot say the same about some of the plugins. Also, customers service is a bit lacking.
author: Cheri R.
pros: Provides maximum flexibility and ease of use for Wordpress specifically. The interface is intuitive, and there is a wide variety of free and paid themes to suit every need.
cons: It doesn't include enough built in functionality for a store. Multiple plugins are required, and they aren't all easy to use. Also, the level of customer service varies a LOT.

review_title: Wordpress is Key!
description: My overall experience has been great! Wordpress is much more official and professional than other free websites.
author: Lyndsey G.
pros: Wordpress allows you to add plugins that ensure your blog posts are meeting all the necessary requirements to get your post high in the search engine.
cons: Wordpress requires frequent updates; however, you can set it up to do this automatically.

review_title: WordPress for the win on my website
description: Overall, I've had a good experience using WP. One of the main reasons I selected to use WP to build my website is due to their ADA plugins. I previously used GoDaddy for my website but could not get what I needed there so moved over to WP. The learning curve is a bit more than what I would like, however, partnering with someone who knows their way around has shortened it quite a bit. I am super pleased with how my website came out and am confident it will support my needs long term. I also like knowing I can renew my subscription for multiple years and not have to worry about it every year. As a small business owner, I enjoy tools that make my life easier.
author: Crystal H.
pros: WordPress sparked my interest based on the robust level of features and mult-year subscription options.
cons: I found the learning curve a bit challenging so much so that I did hire a WP specialist to help me build my website.

review_title: Best platform to build anything you can imagine.
description: Wordpress is amazing I enjoyed a lot when I build websites with it is like building website with drag and drop very easy
author: Abhishek R.
pros: Wordpress is the best platform to build websites, professional morden attractive for free, and the best part it is free, we can create a website without having prior knowledge of coding. Wordpress is very simple and easy to use, you can build anything you can imagine, a blog, a company, an e-commerce store anything you want for free and I build all of my website with WordPress.
cons: I use WordPress as my prior since last 4 year still now I still not face any single problem.

review_title: The King of CMS (If You Don't Mind the Drama)
description: Value for Money - While the WordPress core software is open-source and free, calling it "free" is slightly misleading for a business context. To get a site performing at a professional level, budgets need to be allocated for high-quality hosting, a solid premium theme, and likely a few paid plugins for security or SEO. That said, even with these costs, the value proposition beats closed platforms where subscription fees can hike unexpectedly. You are paying for ownership and freedom. Customer Support - There is no 1-800 number for WordPress.org. For users expecting white-glove service, this can be a shock. However, the community support is the platform's greatest asset. Virtually every error code or conflict has been documented and solved by the community on forums or YouTube. If direct support is a must, it requires paying for a Managed WordPress host rather than relying on the software organization itself. Ease of Use & Page Editing - WordPress is a powerful operating system, not a simple drag-and-drop toy. There is a learning curve. The native Block Editor (Gutenberg) has improved significantly but can still feel clunky compared to proprietary website builders. Many professionals eventually bypass the default editor in favor of third-party page builders. Templates & Website Design - The repository offers thousands of themes, but quality varies wildly. Many "niche" themes look great in demos but break easily. Mobile Responsiveness - Out of the box, modern WordPress themes are fully responsive. Mobile issues rarely stem from the software itself but rather from user implementation—such as using non-responsive tables or oversized images. It handles mobile well, but it requires the user to audit their work on different devices. The "Elephant in the Room" (Company Politics) From a product standpoint, WordPress is solid. However, the recent friction between company leadership and third-party hosting providers has left a sour taste. The software remains excellent, and the community is resilient, but the current political climate at the top adds a layer of uncertainty that wasn't there in previous years. It’s a great product, but the governance drama is a valid concern for new users.
author: Troy C.
pros: Unbeatable ecosystem and flexibility. The community is the best tech support in the world. You own your data completely; no platform lock-in.
cons: Corporate politics and leadership drama are becoming a distraction. Requires a "DIY" mindset; no official support hotline. "Free" can become expensive once you add necessary premium plugins.

review_title: Amazing when you don't have an in-house web developer.
description: Overall, the experience is definitely worth it, especially if you don't have an in-house web developer.
author: Athena A.
pros: It's very easy to learn and train new people on how to edit pages across the site. The block system makes designing new sites simple whenever we need to add something new.
cons: Our company uses a theme, which can be a bit restrictive when attempting to do something creative with custom coding.
author_profession: Administrative Assistant to Technology and Technical Affairs

review_title: In a league of its own
description: I run over 20 WordPress websites, and there is no doubt that Automattic is one of the greatest forces behind the democratisation of the internet. I recommend the GeneratePress template for a clean, flexible build.
author: Neil H.
pros: As one of the few open source content management systems, WordPress is in a different league to other CMS options. Free to download, there is no better value for money. Since the inclusion of Gutenburg, WP has been easier to use. Woo Commerce is a fantastic plugin for ecommerce.
cons: There is a frustrating bug at the moment which is affecting the scheduling of posts. The Gutenburg block system could also be improved with more sophisticated user controls.

review_title: Great visual editor and content manager for websites.
description: Overall, my experience has been mostly positive. WordPress is a powerful platform for designers who want both creative freedom and scalability, but it does require regular maintenance and performance optimization to run smoothly.
author: Christopher T.
pros: As a designer, I appreciate how flexible WordPress is when it comes to customisation. The vast library of themes and plugins allows me to create high quality sites without touching much code, and tools like Elementor or custom CSS give me extra control over the design when I need it.
cons: Sometimes it can feel bloated and clunky, especially when too many plugins are installed—it slows down the site and sometimes leads to conflicts.

review_title: Using WordPress for Chapter & P2P Websites is Easy!
author: Damian W.
pros: Our foundation got WordPress about 10 years ago to build our chapter websites. The look was good and the build was pretty straight forward. Since then are also using WordPress for one of our P2P program websites. The decision to use this, instead of another software we had, was for it's capability to create nice, clean, and stylish pages. We can create multiple templates and the pages are easy to edit. It works well our other software and has a good selection of API plugins. Also, it's cost, is minimal. We only pay for one plugin! Plus, customer support is pretty good. Overall, I would recommend WordPress for those wanting to create website that are easy to build and look great!
cons: Only issue I have seen with WordPress is sometimes the syncing/updating of data on our WP pages, from our source software, can take some time.

review_title: Makes having a website easy
description: We're glad to have a professional looking website without any coding involved. WordPress also provides lots of flexibility and customization with their websites.
author: Johnathan S.
pros: What I love best about Wordpress are the plugins. If there is something you want to be able to do with your website, you can usually find it through a plugin.
cons: Because there is so much you can do with WordPress, sometimes it can be confusing to know where to look to make changes to your website that you are wanting to happen. However this all depends on the type of theme you use.

review_title: Very easy website designing platform
description: Great website building tool, its templates are easy to edit. Definitely value for money. i havent used the customer support as i can find free online videos.
author: Andy N.
pros: Easy-to-use website building tool. Its functionality is excellent. i was able to design my website learning from free videos.
cons: Sometimes it gives me errors, which I gets me stuck in safe mode. As i have used the free version i cannot complain.

review_title: WordPressing a lifetime journey
description: From self taught user to complex international ecommerce project management, diving deep into each core and additional feature, always looking for the best performances and results.
author: Paul B.
pros: I feel it my tool, when challenged asking to solve a problem or what I can achieve with it, I know how to reply and what to do to get the results.
cons: Lack of collaboration; long time planned feature, endless developing and being a fundamental one it's a big missed opportunity for the stack you build with WP.

review_title: Great for beginners!
description: Once you get the hang of it, it's one of the easiest platforms to create a website on for beginners.
author: Franci H.
pros: It was fairly easy to learn to use. There are a lot of online tools available to help you through use of the tool which is very helpful.
cons: It would make it easier if it had a way to tell you if some of the apps conflicted with each other. It would also be helpful if you were given a "list of things you need" when you first get started, like apps that you need that work alongside these types of platforms, like a server for example. These are things you wouldn't think of having to need when first getting started.

review_title: WordPress is a nice resource for Website Creation
description: Overall experience has been positive. It is easy enough to use and love the plug in capabilities. Perhaps the paid version has higher security.
author: Dana M.
pros: WordPress is easy to use and free, if you use it on your own web host. It made creating our company website quite simple, and we have several plugins installed to calculate shipping and take orders (ecommerce).
cons: I am not crazy about the security - it seems to come under attack from spammers and bots more often than it should.

review_title: Introducing WordPress: The worlds Best Website builder and content management system
description: My overall experience with WordPress is superb. As a content management system and a website builder creating a website is fast as the ecosystem has numerous free and premium themes that can be edited by website builders such as Elementor. Its Drag and drop features are also great and users require minimum technical expertise to create a website . Overall i Give it a 10/10 Rating.
author: Dennis M.
pros: What i love most about WordPress is its ability to integrate with numerous plugins such as WooCommerce, Google Sitekit, Elementor thats the best website builder and WP forms.
cons: WordPress meets all my needs thereby there is nothing that i do not like about it as a website builder and a content management system.
